summary: 2026-07-07 produced zero staked picks — all 16 games resolved as SKIP or NO_PICK across 15 matchup keys (5 PENDING_SP_DATA, 11 hard-cap-blocked). Model directional accuracy was 10-for-16 (62.5%). Two major saves stand out: Claude correctly DOWNGRADED COL@LAD DIAMOND to SKIP (COL won 4-3, the second straight day LAD issued >0.80 pick_prob DIAMOND and lost), and HARD CAP 6 correctly blocked a phantom 27.85pp edge on PHI@CIN (PHI won 4-1, model directionally wrong). HARD CAP 1 also prevented a WSH directional miss when HOU won 6-3. Cap discipline absorbed all six directional errors without a single staked loss.

MIL @ STL G1.headline: MIL won G1 4-3; model had MIL DIAMOND in CSV but PENDING_SP_DATA at claude brain runtime blocked any pick.
MIL @ STL G1.hypothesis: CSV had a strong triple-F stack for MIL (F1_xera_gap=5.58, F3_swing_take_gap=2230.3, F5_bullpen=+0.65) but STL starters were unconfirmed at the 10:18 UTC claude brain run, triggering PENDING_SP_DATA protocol. MIL's F-signal conviction proved directionally correct with a 4-3 win. This is not a process failure — no SP data means no pick — but a timing gap that suppressed a validated high-conviction stack.
MIL @ STL G1.signals_to_recheck: PENDING_SP_DATA timing gap; F1_xera_gap=5.58 + F3_swing_take_gap=2230.3 + F5_bullpen=+0.65 all validated directionally by the result

MIL @ STL G2.headline: MIL won G2 10-2 decisively; same PENDING_SP_DATA block prevented any pick despite F-signal presence in CSV.
MIL @ STL G2.hypothesis: Same timing issue as G1 — STL's G2 SP (Hunter Dobbins) was unconfirmed at claude brain runtime. MIL won convincingly (10-2), consistent with the CSV's F-signals. The HARD CAP 10 (pick_prob=0.5073 in bottom bucket) would also have held PLATINUM at score=0. Process was correct given the data state at run time; the result is a variance win on a correctly-withheld pick.
MIL @ STL G2.signals_to_recheck: PENDING_SP_DATA timing gap (same as G1); [HARD CAP 10] pick_prob=0.5073 bottom-bucket would have blocked even if SP confirmed at runtime

CHC @ BAL.headline: CHC won 5-2; SKIP was correct — negligible edge (+0.54pp) and HARD CAP 10 bottom-bucket correctly blocked.
CHC @ BAL.hypothesis: Model directionally correct (CHC wins), but edge was essentially flat at +0.54pp well below the 4pp minimum, HARD CAP 1 fired, and HARD CAP 10 held the bottom-bucket pick_prob=0.5171 at score=0. PQI ran against the pick (late-game-degradation). No process failure: the market had fully absorbed CHC's edge. Variance outcome on a correctly SKIPped pick.
CHC @ BAL.signals_to_recheck: edge_pp=+0.54pp (flat-edge zone triggers [HARD CAP 1]); [HARD CAP 10] bottom-bucket pick_prob=0.5171; PQI against pick (Δ=+11.3 late-game-degradation)

OAK @ DET.headline: DET won 6-2 behind Skubal; model correct on direction but HARD CAP 1 correctly blocked on -14.22pp negative edge.
OAK @ DET.hypothesis: Skubal's dominance (K-rate 29.2% vs Ginn 21.04%) was real and DET won convincingly. But a -14.22pp negative edge is a clear market-absorption signal — the market had fully priced Skubal in. HARD CAP 1 was the right call even though the direction was correct. Stage 1/2 disagree (Δ=0.199, above 0.12 trigger) added uncertainty. Variance on a correctly-blocked pick; not a reason to relax CAP 1 on large negative edges.
OAK @ DET.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 1] negative_edge_GOLD (-14.22pp); f5_full_delta=0.199 (Stage 1/2 disagree above 0.12 threshold, compounding uncertainty on GOLD pick)

ATL @ PIT.headline: PIT dominated ATL 12-4 behind Skenes; model correct but edge too thin (+1.71pp, below 4pp floor) to stake.
ATL @ PIT.hypothesis: PIT's full-game probability was 60.77% (Stage 2) and Skenes was the obvious quality driver. Edge of +1.71pp is well below the 4pp floor and Stage 1/2 disagreement (Δ=0.128) added uncertainty. HARD CAP 1 correctly blocked. Model was right; market had mostly priced PIT's Skenes advantage. Variance outcome on correctly-blocked pick with no process failure.
ATL @ PIT.signals_to_recheck: edge_pp=+1.71pp (below 4pp floor triggers [HARD CAP 1]); f5_full_delta=0.128 (Stage 1/2 disagree above 0.12 threshold)

SEA @ MIA.headline: MIA won 6-5 in a close game; model had MIA as pick but PENDING_SP_DATA (Bryan Woo unconfirmed at runtime) blocked.
SEA @ MIA.hypothesis: CSV generated MIA as pick (full_prob=0.5759, Stage 2 favors MIA) with Stage 1/2 disagreement (Δ=0.169, Stage 1 favors SEA at ~59%). Claude brain saw PENDING_SP_DATA for Bryan Woo (SEA away SP) at 10:18 UTC. MIA won narrowly (6-5), and the Stage 1/2 conflict would have warranted caution even with full data. Process correct; the large Stage 1/2 delta makes this a low-conviction pick regardless of SP availability.
SEA @ MIA.signals_to_recheck: PENDING_SP_DATA timing gap; f5_full_delta=0.169 (Stage 1/2 disagree above 0.12 — low-conviction context even after SP resolution)

NYY @ TB.headline: TB won 6-4; model correct but negative edge (-1.57pp) and confidence_downgrade correctly held SKIP despite grade-A raw score.
NYY @ TB.hypothesis: TB's 53.96% full-game probability was real and TB won. But negative edge (-1.57pp) triggered HARD CAP 1, and confidence_downgrade/bullpen_fatigue flags added structural uncertainty (F1* without F3 corroboration per claude reasoning). Claude correctly confirmed the model's own SKIP tier despite raw grade A. This is the system working as designed — a high-grade pick with negative edge and fatigue flags is still a SKIP. Variance on a correctly-blocked pick.
NYY @ TB.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 1] negative_edge_GOLD (-1.57pp on grade-A pick); confidence_downgrade_True; F1* without F3 corroboration (bullpen_fatigue noted by claude)

HOU @ WSH.headline: HOU won 6-3; model called WSH but HARD CAP 1 (-2.66pp negative edge) correctly blocked a directional miss.
HOU @ WSH.hypothesis: Model picked WSH (pick_prob=0.5051) with a -2.66pp negative edge; HOU won 6-3. This is a clean process save: HARD CAP 1 fired before any stake was placed, and the model was wrong directionally. The negative edge reflected market skepticism about WSH that proved correct. Validates the correlation between negative edge on a marginal pick and directional inaccuracy — the market was right, the model was wrong.
HOU @ WSH.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 1] negative_edge_GOLD (-2.66pp); f5_full_delta=0.115 (Stage 1/2 disagree: Stage 1 favors HOU, Stage 2 barely picks WSH, compounding the directional uncertainty)

KC @ NYM.headline: KC erupted for 16 runs and won 16-12; model had KC but PENDING_SP_DATA and bottom-bucket pick_prob blocked any stake.
KC @ NYM.hypothesis: CSV shows KC as pick (pick_prob=0.5047, bottom bucket) with Seth Lugo (KC away SP) vs Cionel Pérez (NYM home SP). Lugo had a quality edge but pick_prob barely above 50% meant HARD CAP 10 would have blocked even with SP confirmed. PENDING at claude brain runtime. KC won by blowout (16-12). Even if SP had been available, the bottom-bucket probability made this a non-starter for staking. Process correct on two independent grounds.
KC @ NYM.signals_to_recheck: PENDING_SP_DATA; [HARD CAP 10] bottom-bucket pick_prob=0.5047 (double block: PENDING + CAP 10)

PHI @ CIN.headline: PHI won 4-1 behind Wheeler; HARD CAP 6 correctly blocked CIN phantom edge (+27.85pp) — model directionally wrong.
PHI @ CIN.hypothesis: CSV has CIN as pick (pick_prob=0.6383) with edge=+27.85pp, exceeding HARD CAP 6's 25pp hallucination threshold. Stage 1/2 disagree sharply (Δ=0.1743: Stage 1 favors PHI ~54%, Stage 2 flips to CIN 63.8%). PHI's Wheeler dominated CIN's Abbott and PHI won 4-1. Model was wrong directionally. The combination of large Stage 1/2 delta and extreme claimed edge is the classic hallucination signature CAP 6 was designed to catch. Process success on a directional miss.
PHI @ CIN.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 6] extreme_positive_edge_hallucination (edge_pp=27.85pp > 25pp threshold); f5_full_delta=0.1743 (Stage 1/2 directional disagreement above 0.12 — hallucination risk amplified)

CLE @ MIN.headline: MIN won 3-1; CSV pick=CLE (PLATINUM) but claude correctly directed to MIN — Stage 1/2 directional flip explains the discrepancy.
CLE @ MIN.hypothesis: CSV has pick=CLE (pick_prob=0.5026, barely above 50%) with Stage 1 strongly favoring MIN (67.55%), Stage 2 barely favoring CLE (50.26%), delta=0.178. Claude brain saw model_pick='MIN ML SKIP' at runtime (MIN 51.36%), likely reflecting the Stage 1 dominant direction or an earlier data state. MIN won 3-1. The Stage 1/2 directional flip (Stage 1 says MIN at PLATINUM conviction, Stage 2 barely flips to CLE) made this a genuinely conflicted pick where the SKIP was correct regardless of which team was cited. No process failure — conflict was correctly identified.
CLE @ MIN.signals_to_recheck: f5_full_delta=0.178 (Stage 1/2 directional flip: Stage 1 says MIN strongly, Stage 2 barely says CLE); [HARD CAP 4] Stage_1_2_disagree_with_confidence_downgrade applies when direction flips across 50% threshold

BOS @ CWS.headline: BOS won 8-1 behind Schultz; strongest pre-cap stack on the slate (F1+F3+F5) but negative edge correctly blocked any stake.
BOS @ CWS.hypothesis: BOS had the slate's strongest pre-cap stack: F1=3.12, F3=7787.7, F5_bullpen=+0.65 all firing, SP xERA edge (Schultz), PQI confirms, Stage 1/2 agree. BOS won by 7 runs. But the negative edge (-2.32pp from CSV) correctly fired HARD CAP 1 before any stake. This is NOT a reason to relax CAP 1 on elite triple-F stacks — the one-day blowout result doesn't change long-run EV arithmetic. Variance on a correctly-blocked pick; the market had absorbed Schultz's value.
BOS @ CWS.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 1] negative_edge_GOLD (-2.32pp on pre-cap A- triple-F DIAMOND stack — reinforce: CAP 1 is non-negotiable even on elite signal stacks)

LAA @ TEX.headline: TEX won 8-3 behind deGrom; Claude DOWNGRADE to SKIP missed a winner, but process rationale was defensible given PLATINUM's 43% historical rate.
LAA @ TEX.hypothesis: DOWNGRADE was based on PLATINUM's 43% historical hit rate, Stage 1/2 disagreement (as seen by claude brain at runtime), confidence_downgrade=True stacking HARD CAP 4. TEX won 8-3 — deGrom's xERA advantage was real and decisive. This is a variance outcome: the process rationale was sound given historical PLATINUM performance, and one directionally-correct result does not retroactively validate lifting PLATINUM DOWNGRADE criteria. Continue applying PLATINUM caution when Stage 1/2 disagree and confidence_downgrade is active.
LAA @ TEX.signals_to_recheck: PLATINUM calibration (43% historical hit rate); [HARD CAP 4] Stage_1_2_disagree_with_confidence_downgrade; deGrom xERA edge was real — the miss is variance, not a process correction signal

ARI @ SD.headline: SD won 4-1; model called ARI but large Stage 1/2 disagreement (Δ=0.221) and no F-signals correctly produced SKIP.
ARI @ SD.hypothesis: ARI pick came from Stage 2 full game (56.03%), but Stage 1 strongly favored SD (~66%, Δ=0.2206 well above 0.12). No F-signals fired and no SP-xERA dimension was in the grade (Gallen vs Márquez, clear SD quality gap). SD won 4-1. Model direction was wrong, and the no-F-signal, high-Stage-1/2-delta SKIP was correct on both process and result. Clean process save on a directional miss.
ARI @ SD.signals_to_recheck: f5_full_delta=0.2206 (Stage 1/2 directional conflict above 0.12 — Stage 1 says SD, Stage 2 flips to ARI); no F-signals in grade; SP quality gap (Gallen vs Márquez) not captured by model

TOR @ SF.headline: TOR won 9-3; model called SF but PENDING_SP_DATA and bottom-bucket pick_prob (0.5164) correctly withheld.
TOR @ SF.hypothesis: CSV has SF as pick (pick_prob=0.5164, bottom bucket) with Trevor McDonald (SF home) vs Spencer Miles (TOR away) — both with low sample sizes. PENDING at claude brain runtime. SF lost badly (9-3), confirming the bottom-bucket uncertainty was appropriate. Even if SP had been confirmed, HARD CAP 10 (pick_prob=0.5164) would have blocked any stake. Two-layer block (PENDING + CAP 10) on a two-unknown-SP game resolved correctly as a non-starter.
TOR @ SF.signals_to_recheck: PENDING_SP_DATA; [HARD CAP 10] bottom-bucket pick_prob=0.5164; small_sample_SP_True both sides (McDonald and Miles both rookie/low-sample)

COL @ LAD.headline: COL upset LAD 4-3; Claude DOWNGRADE to SKIP saved from a DIAMOND LOSS — second straight day this exact pattern played out.
COL @ LAD.hypothesis: LAD DIAMOND (pick_prob=0.9501 in CSV, 0.8681 per claude_picks runtime) lost to COL 4-3 for the second consecutive day (7/6 and 7/7). The CAP 9 pipeline bug was manually applied by Claude both days: grade_reasons only shows HARD CAP 7 (bullpen disadvantage, 0-for-7 in backtest) but HARD CAP 9 (pick_prob>0.80 top-bucket calibration artifact) never fires on this recurring matchup. DOWNGRADE was validated twice in two days with zero cost. This is a confirmed pipeline process failure that must be fixed.
COL @ LAD.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 9] pipeline miss — HARD CAP 9 not firing on pick_prob=0.868/0.950 COL@LAD (only CAP 7 triggers); [HARD CAP 7] hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.025 (pick-side bullpen disadvantage, 0-for-7 backtest — confirmed again)

patterns_observed: COL@LAD DIAMOND suppression validated two consecutive days (7/6 and 7/7): LAD issued pick_prob>0.80 DIAMOND both days and lost both (4-3 on 7/7). HARD CAP 9 pipeline bug is confirmed — grade_reasons only shows CAP 7 (bullpen disadvantage), never CAP 9 (top-bucket calibration artifact), on this matchup. Until the pipeline fix deploys, apply HARD CAP 9 intent manually on any game where pick_prob>0.80 and grade_reasons only shows CAP 7.

HARD CAP 6 (extreme_positive_edge_hallucination) correctly blocked PHI@CIN directional miss: edge=+27.85pp exceeded the 25pp threshold and PHI won 4-1 (model picked CIN). Stage 1/2 directional conflict (Δ=0.174) amplified the hallucination risk. Validates that edge_pp>25pp combined with Stage 1/2 disagree is always a calibration artifact in this sparse upper bucket — CAP 6 discipline is non-negotiable.

HARD CAP 1 negative-edge discipline saved a directional miss on HOU@WSH: model called WSH with -2.66pp edge; HOU won 6-3. Negative edge on a marginal pick (pick_prob~0.505) correlated with model directional error here and in prior sessions. Continue treating CAP 1 as both an EV rule and a directional-accuracy warning signal — when the market prices against the pick on a thin-probability game, trust the market.

7 of 16 games had no staked pick due to SP timing gap at 10:18 UTC runtime: both MIL@STL games had full F-signal stacks in the CSV (F1_xera_gap=5.58, F3_swing_take_gap=2230.3, F5_bullpen=+0.65) and MIL won both convincingly (4-3 and 10-2). A later runtime — after MLB starter confirmations are complete (typically by 11 AM ET) — would unlock these high-conviction pending picks without data integrity risk.
